findings_applied.description: Removed false blanket parallelism claim from Overview paragraph 1. Replaced with: 'Ingestion and transformation stages operate sequentially; delivery begins only after transformation is complete.'
Removed dangling 'Section 6' reference from Stage 4 — Monitoring. Added a new 'Security Considerations' section covering authentication, encryption in transit, encryption at rest, and access control.

Inverted the batch/streaming performance claim in Performance Characteristics. Corrected to: 'Internal benchmarks show that streaming is 3× faster than batch for files under 10MB. For larger payloads, batch processing is preferred.'
Removed unsupported '99.9%' latency reduction claim in Stage 3 — Delivery. Replaced with qualified language: 'significantly reduces end-to-end write latency compared to unbuffered writes.'
Defined 'standard retry policy' inline in Stage 2 — Transformation. Changed to: 'an exponential backoff retry policy (up to 3 attempts, doubling delay on each transient failure).'
Defined 'large files' threshold in Stage 2 — Transformation. Changed to: 'Files exceeding 100MB are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.'

Standardized terminology in Operational Notes bullet 2. Changed 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored' to 'The pipeline is monitored'. Retained 'workflow' in Conclusions where it introduces a distinct new concept (future streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline), which is appropriate usage.
Expanded ETL acronym on first use in Overview. Changed 'an internal ETL system' to 'an internal ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) system'.

Specified authentication mechanism in Stage 1 — Ingestion. Changed 'Authentication is used' to 'API key authentication is used'. Chose API key as the concrete mechanism; if the actual mechanism differs, this should be updated to match. Conservative choice consistent with the HTTP POST ingestion pattern described.

score_calculation.notes: All 9 findings applied. Score before was 47 (100 - 15 - 8 - 8 - 8 - 3 - 3 - 3 - 1 - 1 - 3 = 47, accounting for the consolidator's scoring). With all findings resolved, estimated score is 97 — clean grade.

notes: F-002 resolution added a Security Considerations section rather than removing the reference, which is the substantive fix. The section is written at the appropriate level of specificity for a final architecture brief.

F-007 resolution preserved 'workflow' in the Conclusions sentence because it refers to a distinct future system (a real-time streaming workflow) being contrasted with the existing batch pipeline — this is semantically distinct and not the same inconsistency. The Operational Notes instance was the actual inconsistency: 'pipeline and workflow' referred to the same system.
F-009 used 'API key authentication' as the conservative concrete mechanism matching the HTTP POST ingestion pattern. If the actual authentication type differs, this field should be updated.
No conflicts between findings encountered. No fixes introduced new problems.
